findings.description: subprocess.run with shell=True builds a command from untrusted job payload source/dest fields, enabling arbitrary command injection.
attempt is reset to 0 at the top of every loop body before incrementing, so the counter never accumulates and a job that always returns falsy without raising loops forever.
Because attempt is reset to 0 each iteration, the backoff RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS*(attempt+1) is always 2.0 and never escalates as intended.
poll_loop is async but calls blocking time.sleep instead of await asyncio.sleep, freezing the entire event loop and starving other coroutines.
run_with_retries is a synchronous, sleep-bearing call invoked inside the async poll_loop, blocking the event loop for the full retry/backoff duration.
Bare except Exception swallows all errors with no classification, so non-retryable failures (e.g. bad payload) are retried and contribute to the broken-counter spin.
sum(values) does not validate element types; non-numeric values from the JSON payload raise TypeError, which is then swallowed by the bare except and feeds the infinite-retry path.
pop(0) on a list is O(n); a collections.deque would give O(1) dequeue for the queue.
Unknown job kind returns False, which is indistinguishable from a genuine execution failure and triggers MAX_RETRIES of pointless retries before being marked permanently failed.
asyncio is imported but asyncio.sleep is never used, signalling the missing non-blocking sleep in the async loop.
